---
title: "Barplot motif analyses hPu hPy S1-ENDseq"
output: 
date: "2026-02-20"
---

######################################################################################################
# Gustave Roussy (GR) | CNRS UMR 9019, Genome dynamics and instability Lab - Gabriel MATOS RODRIGUES 
# Developed by BEKKOUCHE Louisa - bekkouche.louisa@gmail.com                                 
# Edited by: Genome dynamics and instability Lab                                     
# v.0.1 - 2025/12/18 (for Human genome) | v.0.2 2026/02/16 (for Mouse genenome)	     
# R version 4.5.2 (2025-10-31)                                                       
# MIT licence - Copyright (c) 2025 Gabriel Matos Rodrigues			                     
# run it in a env conda for R version and enjoy your results! :)                     
#######################################################################################################

```{r}

#######################################################################################################

# Peak count comparison between two BED datasets based on repeat-motif classification
# - Loads two BED files (Control and Case)
# - Extracts the motif column (column 14)
# - Classifies motifs into grouped categories
# - Counts the number of peaks per motif group and per condition
# - Generates a barplot comparing conditions
# - Exports the plot as a PDF

#######################################################################################################

# Packages installation CRAN + BIOCONDUCTOR

cran_pkgs <- c("ggplot2","dplyr","readr","ggthemes")

# Utility function: installs only missing packages
install_missing <- function(pkgs) {
    missing <- pkgs[!pkgs %in% installed.packages()[, "Package"]]
    if (length(missing) > 0) {
        install.packages(missing, repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")
    }
}

# Install missing CRAN packages
install_missing(cran_pkgs)

message("All required packages are installed.")

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(ggthemes)

#######################################################################################################

# User input paths
# BED files containing at least 14 columns (column 14 = motif)
bed1 <- "put the ctrl bed file path"   # Control dataset
bed2 <- "put the case bed file path"   # Case dataset

# Condition labels
cond1 <- "DMSO"   # control condition
cond2 <- "BQQ"    # treated condition

# Load BED files
# No header; tab-separated; motif must be column 14
df1 <- read.table(bed1, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
df2 <- read.table(bed2, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Assigning column 14 as the motif column
colnames(df1)[14] <- "motif"
colnames(df2)[14] <- "motif"

# Define motif grouping scheme
group1 <- c("(GA)n", "(AG)n", "(TC)n", "(CT)n")
group2 <- c("(GAA)n", "(AAG)n", "(AGA)n", "(TCT)n", "(TTC)n", "(CTT)n")
group3 <- c("(GAAA)n","(AAAG)n","(AAGA)n","(AGAA)n",
            "(TTTC)n","(CTTT)n","(TCTT)n","(TTCT)n")
group4 <- c("(GGAA)n","(GAAG)n","(AAGG)n","(AGGA)n",
            "(TTCC)n","(CCTT)n","(TCCT)n","(CTTC)n",
            "(GAAAA)n","(AAAAG)n","(AAAGA)n","(AAGAA)n","(AGAAA)n",
            "(CTTTT)n","(TTTTC)n","(TTTCT)n","(TTCTT)n","(TCTTT)n")

# Function to classify motifs
# Adds a new column "category" based on motif identity
classify <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    mutate(category =
             case_when(
               motif %in% group1 ~ "(GA)n/(TC)n",
               motif %in% group2 ~ "(GAA)n/(TTC)n",
               motif %in% group3 ~ "(GAAA)n/(TTTC)n",
               motif %in% group4 ~ "(GGAA)n/(TTCC)n",
               TRUE ~ "Other"   # Any motif not included in the defined groups
             ))
}

# Apply motif classification to both conditions
df1 <- classify(df1)
df2 <- classify(df2)

# Count peaks per motif category
count1 <- df1 %>% count(category) %>% mutate(condition = cond1)
count2 <- df2 %>% count(category) %>% mutate(condition = cond2)

# Combine both datasets for plotting
df_all <- bind_rows(count1, count2)

# Ordering categories for the plot
df_all$category <- factor(
  df_all$category,
  levels = c(
    "Other",
    "(GGAA)n/(TTCC)n",
    "(GAAA)n/(TTTC)n",
    "(GAA)n/(TTC)n",
    "(GA)n/(TC)n"
  )
)

# Barplot comparing motif counts
p <- ggplot(df_all, aes(x = n, y = category, fill = condition)) +
  geom_bar(
    stat = "identity",
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.75),
    width = 0.6,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    x = "Number of Peaks",
    y = "",
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  # Define colors for each condition
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("DMSO" = "black", "BQQ" = "red"),
    breaks = c("DMSO", "BQQ")
  ) +
  theme(
    text = element_text(size = 13),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 8),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 8)
  )

print(p)

# Export plot as PDF
ggsave(
  "put the output folder path",
  p,
  width = 5,
  height = 4
)

#######################################################################################################

```
